select_when: The decision is not yes-or-no but more-likely-or-less-likely, so binary framing would hide meaningful uncertainty.
The team needs to compare ranges, distributions, or expected value instead of treating one forecast as certain.
Uncertainty can be reduced through better priors, disconfirming evidence, or explicit probability ranges rather than by telling a cleaner story.
The current argument sounds precise, but the real task is to quantify how much confidence the team actually has.
avoid_when: The work requires a concrete commitment now, but the team is using probability language to postpone choosing after the key economics are already clear.
The probability estimates are being treated as exact-looking numbers without enough evidence, base-rate discipline, or sensitivity testing behind them.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on uncertainty ranges, expected value, and disciplined probability communication rather than broad statistics survey content.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style forecasting overlays because the raw source is already explicit on expected value, error discipline, and uncertainty communication.
Did not add higher-order composition fields because this batch stops at the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The avoid-when language is conservative reviewed normalization over a source that is stronger on false precision than on explicit anti-activation routing.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit that probabilistic thinking is strongest when binary framing fails and uncertainty ranges matter; review mainly compacts those bullets into routing triggers.

avoid_when.note: The source is explicit about false precision and misuse of exact-looking estimates; the commitment-delay warning is conservative reviewed normalization.

input_type.note: The raw model activates on uncertainty-heavy choices, but the compact type label is reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The raw source produces expected-value and probability-range reasoning; the output label compresses that into a typed judgment artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Probabilistic thinking is clearly probabilistic and metacognitive in use, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
